pages.full_text:
water comes up. It's surrounded by steep, smooth rocks & a pale man fell down & landed on his [illegible] & became even paler. He & his wife immediately lit cigarettes.
   We walked back to the van & stopped in a valley by a draughty stream for lunch. Thomas, Chris, John, Grandpa, Grandma & Helen walked across a hill, & Simon drove the rest of us the long way to a farm to meet them. At the end of the walk Thomas, Tim, John & Eve forded a stream on stepping stones. The others took the long route back by the road.
   We stopped at shops & farms for milk, but there wasn't any. So we're having milk water with our cereal tomorrow.
   Thomas & Eve + John & Charles had showers in the morning. When we got back to the campsite, Chris, William, Peter & Justin had showers. It almost rained as we had supper at 8.0.
   Sat Mon 30
   Chris had an extra blanket last night & was finally warm enough, but she decided to leave at 10.30 with Grandpa, Grandma & Aunt Helen, to go home by bus from Kidderminster, because of her bad cold.
   Simon, Thomas, John & Charles have gone off for a walk. Peter is reading, William revising, Justin playing cards & Eve about to write letters. We have watched more shaving. The men are learning. The [?nice] daughter [illegible]
pages.notes: Page begins mid-sentence — continuation from previous spread. No handwritten page number visible in top-left corner. The date heading 'Sat Mon 30' is unusual — possibly 'Sat May 30' misread, or a different abbreviation; the word after 'Sat' could be 'Mon' or 'May'. A word after 'landed on his' is illegible — possibly 'foot' or 'bottom'. The last two lines of the page are partially obscured/cut off; '[?nice] daughter [illegible]' is the best reading available. 'Shaving' context unclear — possibly watching someone shave, or a misread word. The year cannot be determined from this page; 'Mon 30' or 'May 30' does not narrow the year with certainty. Thomas, Chris, John, Grandpa, Grandma, Helen, Simon, Tim, Eve, Charles, William, Peter, Justin are family members. Kidderminster is in Worcestershire, England.

[Food log — left column]
Fri   creips, pork pies, toms

      afr sponge.

      [struck: apples] [struck: pears] [struck: bananas]

      [struck: pears] apples, choc bars.

Sun

      [struck: stewed apples] skotbread
      [struck: marg strudlorige] stewed apple

Mon
am    h.b. eggs, salad,
      maconese.
      [struck: pears] apples [?/cherries]
      [illegible].

Mon
pm    cheese, salad
      rice pud.

Tues
am    corned beef,
      dried fr.

[Narrative — right column]
Simon, Justin, Thomas & William played cricket in the afternoon warmer
John collected sheeps wool, & Eve, sheep shit. After tea, Eve had a row with [?Abt.] Simon, Eve, Thomas, John & Charles set off on a circuitous route to the Mountain Centre. Charles — "what is a oner." John "A twooer is a tine, a threeer a triangle." At the Mountain Centre we met up with Peter & had delicious icecreams. After supper, the boys & Simon played
pages.notes: Page number 42 is visible in top-right corner. The page is divided into two sections: a food/meal log on the left half (two columns: day/meal-time and food items), and narrative text on the right half. Many items in the food log are struck through (indicated with [struck:]). 'afr sponge' likely means 'after sponge' (a type of sponge cake). 'kiche' is likely 'quiche'. 'maconese' is likely 'mayonnaise'. 'skotbread' is likely 'shortbread'. 'pistacios' is the writer's spelling of 'pistachios'. '[?ct. ch]' in Sun am is partially legible — possibly 'cottage cheese'. '[?/cherries]' — the word after apples is uncertain, could be 'cherries' or another fruit. 'icecreams' written as one word. The word after 'a row with' is abbreviated — reads [?Abt.] but could be a name abbreviation. 'sheeps wool' and 'sheep shit' written as found — humorous family observation. 'circuitous' route to the Mountain Centre — 'Mountain Centre' likely refers to a visitor/outdoor centre in a mountainous area of Britain; no further location identifier on this page. The narrative ends mid-sentence — continues on next spread.
